title: Fix handling of --restore shadow-utils
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

#### How to verify it

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago PTAL, I am a little concerned about the test.

> My goal is to catch changes that can end up with a bad image, when users checkin fixes to the contrib Dockerfiles.

Thank you, I had not understood that. Given that, it doesn't sound like this is a good candidate for the BATS tests which run on all arches, adding more opportunities for flaking.

Would it make sense to add this to the `smoke` CI target? That runs once only, builds a binary. There could be a check such as:
```
if (any files have been touched under contrib/buildahimage) then
    for each modified subdirectory
        buildah bud, buildah from, buildah run rpm -qV
```
SGTM, could you send me a patch that does this?
@rhatdan, submitted as https://github.com/rhatdan/buildah/pull/12 (it took slightly longer than I had predicted)

Oooops, sorry, I didn't notice this: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/.cirrus.yml#L101

For a PR like this one, with every Containerfile changing, I think we need 25-30m. OTOH that seems counter to the purpose of a smoke test, which one would want to fail fast. OT3H I'm reluctant to make this a separate Cirrus task all on its own. I think bumping up the smoke timeout is the least-awful approach. @rhatdan @nalind @cevich WDYT?
Should we only run the smoke test if the ~/contrib/buildahimage directory changed?

No, the smoke test does much much much more: 
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/3821391ea6fa0d2a5360bfc6da9fcf9e29133c72/contrib/cirrus/test.sh#L22-L37
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/3821391ea6fa0d2a5360bfc6da9fcf9e29133c72/Makefile#L105-L110
@edigaryev I meant this (The buildahimage) smoke test.
Oh! It does that already: it only runs if there are changed files under `contrib/buildahimages`. If there are none, the test exits within milliseconds.
Excellent, then I would say lets increase the timeout.

Smoke [ran and passed](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4666609362010112?logs=validate_test#L122) (in just over 10 minutes, reminding me that I'm awful at estimating run times). The log confirms that the script tested `centos7`, `stable`, `testing`, and `upstream`; and the timestamps confirm that each took a reasonable time (~1m, although upstream took 3). I call this good.
I have no problem with bumping up the timeout a bit.
LGTM but I'd like one more set of eyeballs on the new script and CI addition

A little more description would be nice so the poor release notes writer could write this up more easily would be nice.
I've tired eyes too, so figure between @edsantiago and I, we've one good pair.
LGTM
